Title: Characterization of two O-methyltransferases involved in the biosynthesis of O-methylated catechins in tea plant

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Jinxuan; Jinxuan × Zijuan F1 population; 27 tea germplasms
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Flavonol and anthocyanin O-methyltransferase (FAOMT)-like, cation-dependent O-methyltransferase; Rossmann-fold methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from S-adenosyl-L-methionine to the 3''-hydroxyl of the galloyl moiety of EGCG, producing EGCG3''Me; also methylates ECG at the 3'' position in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improved stability, lipid solubility, oral bioavailability, and stronger bioactivity than EGCG
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Young leaves, flower buds, mature leaves, flowers, old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control gene for EGCG3''Me accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in young leaves, followed by flower buds; much lower in mature leaves, flowers, old leaves, roots, and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in young leaves, followed by flower buds; low in roots and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis and accumulation of EGCG3''Me in tea plant
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSR-Seq, RNA-seq; NCBI BioProject PRJNA856047; PRJNA855300
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Requires Mg2+ for activity. Crystal structure and mutagenesis identified L50 and Y184 as key residues affecting positional specificity; Y184 is crucial for 3''-O-methylation specificity. Genotype explains >87% of EGCG3''Me variation in spring and autumn in the F1 population.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for breeding or engineering tea cultivars with high EGCG3''Me content by selecting elite high-expression alleles or stacking with CsFAOMT2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsFAOMT1 expression is strongly positively correlated with EGCG3''Me levels across tissues, F1 individuals, and diverse tea germplasms. 2. Recombinant CsFAOMT1 specifically catalyzes 3''-O-methylation of EGCG to generate EGCG3''Me in a Mg2+-dependent manner. 3. Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by EGCG feeding produced EGCG3''Me in vivo. 4. CsFAOMT1 genotype explains most phenotypic variation of EGCG3''Me in the mapping population.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSR-Seq mapping; correlation analysis by RNA-seq and qRT-PCR; recombinant enzyme assay with UPLC and LC-MS/MS; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with EGCG feeding; crystal structure; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter genotyped in tea; CaMV 35S promoter for transient tobacco expression

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Jinxuan; Jinxuan × Zijuan F1 population; 27 tea germplasms
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Flavonol and anthocyanin O-methyltransferase (FAOMT)-like, cation-dependent O-methyltransferase; Rossmann-fold methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from S-adenosyl-L-methionine predominantly to the 4''-hydroxyl of the galloyl moiety of EGCG to form EGCG4''Me; also has lower 3''-O-methyltransferase activity and can generate trace dimethylated EGCG products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improved stability, lipid solubility, oral bioavailability, and stronger bioactivity than EGCG
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Young leaves and other tissues correlated with transcript abundance
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control gene for EGCG4''Me accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Positively correlated with EGCG4''Me accumulation in F1 individuals, germplasms, and different tissues; present only in EGCG4''Me-containing accessions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in tea plants/tissues accumulating EGCG4''Me
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis and accumulation of EGCG4''Me, with EGCG4''Me as the dominant product
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; NCBI BioProject PRJNA855300; structure data PDB 8GXN
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Isolated only from tea plants containing EGCG4''Me and absent from reported reference genomes used by authors. Higher affinity and catalytic efficiency toward EGCG than CsFAOMT1. Key residues include M50, V49, and R184 influencing 4'' specificity and dimethylated product formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for breeding or engineering tea cultivars with high EGCG4''Me content; can be combined with CsFAOMT1 to increase total O-methylated catechins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsFAOMT2 is present only in EGCG4''Me-containing tea plants and its transcript level positively correlates with EGCG4''Me accumulation. 2. Recombinant CsFAOMT2 predominantly catalyzes 4''-O-methylation of EGCG, with lower 3''-O-methylation activity. 3. CsFAOMT2 can also produce trace dimethylated EGCG derivatives. 4. Structural and mutagenesis analyses identified R184 and nearby residues as determinants of 4'' positional specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation analysis by qRT-PCR/RNA-seq; presence/absence analysis in F1 and germplasms; recombinant enzyme assay with UPLC and LC-MS/MS; kinetic analysis; crystal structure; site-directed mutagenesis
